votes.reason: The Python list comprehension exactly mirrors the Coq map over the sequence of indices, correctly computing the derivative by multiplying each element by its index starting from 1.
The Python code correctly computes the expected list using a list comprehension over the range from 1 to len(xs), which exactly matches the Coq map over seq 1 (length xs - 1).

The Python code correctly implements the list transformation defined in the Coq spec, using range(1, len(xs)) to match the indices generated by seq 1 (length xs - 1) and multiplying each element by its index.

Both compute the derivative by multiplying each coefficient xs[i] by its index i for i from 1 to len(xs)-1. The Coq version uses seq 1 (length xs - 1) which generates [1, 2, ..., length xs - 1], and maps each i to nth i xs 0 * i. The Python version iterates i from 1 to len(xs)-1 and computes xs[i] * i. These produce the same list of values for all concrete inputs.

Both compute the derivative by multiplying each coefficient xs[i] by its index i for i from 1 to len(xs)-1. The Coq version uses seq 1 (length xs - 1) which generates [1, 2, ..., length xs - 1], and maps each i to nth i xs 0 * i. The Python version iterates i from 1 to len(xs)-1 and computes xs[i] * i. These produce the same list of values for all concrete inputs.

The Coq spec computes `map (fun i => nth i xs 0 * i) (seq 1 (length xs - 1))`. For a list xs of length n, `seq 1 (n-1)` produces [1, 2, ..., n-1]. `nth i xs 0` gets the i-th element (0-indexed). The Python code computes `[xs[i] * i for i in range(1, len(xs))]`, which iterates i from 1 to len(xs)-1, accessing xs[i] and multiplying by i. These produce the same results for all concrete inputs.
Both compute [xs[i]*i for i from 1 to len(xs)-1] and compare to result; handling of empty/short lists and indexing matches Coq’s seq and nth behavior.
Both compute [xs[i] * i] for i from 1 to len(xs)-1; empty for len(xs) <= 1. Indices and products match Coq nth and seq behavior.
Both compute [xs[i]*i for i from 1 to len(xs)-1], with empty list when len(xs) ≤ 1; indexing and results match.
